text.title: Beta Cohort Communication Cadence Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Beta Cohort Communication Cadence application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Send a weekly broadcast: what shipped, what's coming, what we need from you. ≤300 words. Same day + same time each week.
rule.rationale: Cohorts decay without cadence; predictability creates the open-the-email habit.

rule.statement: ≥90% of cohort completes ≥1 targeted feedback (Loom, form, call) per month. Track per-user completion; chase the missing 10%.
rule.rationale: Cohorts where most users never give feedback are pre-churn cohorts; targeted ask is the leading indicator.

rule.statement: Define graduation criteria up front: minimum usage, ≥1 feedback session, opted into launch tier. Graduation = beta ends, paid plan begins.
rule.rationale: Open-ended betas drift to zombie; explicit graduation creates a natural commercial transition.

rule.statement: Public credit for beta users: changelog mentions, beta-tester badge, early-adopter discount lock-in. Trust is built by visible recognition.
rule.rationale: Public credit converts beta cohort into testimonial source + word-of-mouth distribution.

rule.statement: Post-graduation, beta alumni get: lifetime founder discount, dedicated Slack channel, early access to next features. Alumni become permanent feedback loop.
rule.rationale: Beta cohort is the most informed user base; losing them at graduation is wasteful.
